archetype: Archetype 4 (Swiss & High-Contrast) combined with Archetype 2 (Performance Pro) for a tactical, scientific laboratory aesthetic.
reasoning: A dark, highly technical UI is ideal for laboratory environments where contrast and legibility of critical numbers (countdown timers) are paramount. The use of deep obsidian backgrounds with pure white text and stark cyan/amber accents mimics scientific instrumentation.

guidelines: Use JetBrains Mono for all timers, sample IDs, and numeric metrics.
Use IBM Plex Sans for headings and reading text.
Apply tight tracking to large headings to maintain a dense, technical feel.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Tailwind standard (p-2, p-4, p-6, p-8). Generous vertical padding for scrolling lists, tight padding within data cards.

ExperimentCard.description: The main component for tracking active experiments.
style_rules: Flat solid background (bg-zinc-900), no drop shadows, 1px solid border (border-zinc-800).
Layout: Vertical stack. Header (Sample ID + Condition Label) -> Body (Researcher Name, Start Date) -> Footer (Large Mono Timer + Progress Bar).
Progress bar: Slim (h-2), full width, colored based on time remaining (Cyan -> Amber -> Red).
Removed State: Grayscale, opacity-50, with 'REMOVED' stamp.

PrimaryButton.description: Main Call to Action (Start Experiment, Confirm Removal).
style_rules: Sharp corners (rounded-sm) to match technical feel.
Hover/Active state must maintain high contrast.
Floating labels or distinct overline labels.
Glassmorphism: backdrop-blur-xl bg-zinc-950/80, top border border-zinc-800.
Icons only + subtle label for active state.

interactions_and_motion.hover_states: Buttons and cards should slightly lighten on hover (bg-zinc-800).
interactions_and_motion.active_states: Tap targets must have immediate visual feedback (scale down 0.98 or bg-zinc-700).
interactions_and_motion.auto_clear: When an experiment is marked as 'Removed', transition opacity to 50%, hold for 2 minutes, then slide out gracefully.
interactions_and_motion.accessibility: Use aria-live regions for timer completions. Ensure all inputs have associated labels.

app_background.description: Abstract dark mode background for login or empty states.

experiment_sample_1.description: Close up of a metallic sample undergoing testing.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is a mobile-first web application (React Native Expo style UI). Ensure the main container is restricted in width (max-w-md) and centered on desktop to emulate a mobile screen.
Install Google Fonts 'IBM Plex Sans' and 'JetBrains Mono' for typography.
Implement Lucide-React or Phosphor Icons for the bottom navigation and UI elements.
Use Shadcn UI components for Forms, Inputs, Selects, Dialogs, but customize them heavily to remove the default rounded Vercel look. Make them flat and sharp.
For the countdown logic, use a reliable React hook that updates every second.
Implement local notifications using standard web APIs (or mock them for the browser environment).
Add 'data-testid' attributes to all interactive elements.
